title.rendered: AMI to Host Fall 2018 UEFI Plugfest with Other UEFI Members in Taipei, Taiwan from October 15 -19, 2018

content.rendered:
NORCROSS, GEORGIA: – AMI, a global leader in BIOS and UEFI firmware, server and remote management tools, data storage products and unique solutions based on the Linux® and Android™ operating systems, will be hosting the Fall 2018 UEFI Plugfest with other UEFI members in Taipei, Taiwan from October 15-19, 2018.

The UEFI Forum is a non-profit industry standards body that champions firmware innovation and interoperability through industry collaboration. The UEFI Forum is responsible for promoting UEFI specifications and organizes plugfests throughout the year for UEFI members to test the latest UEFI implementations on their platforms. This year, the Fall 2018 UEFI Plugfest will be held in Taipei, Taiwan from October 15-19 at the Capital Hotel – Dazhi.

At UEFI Plugfests, members can test their solutions on various platforms and OS versions, as well as on Host Bus Adapter (HBA) plug-in cards from independent hardware vendors (IHVs). Aside from the testing sessions, members also have the opportunity to attend technical sessions covering firmware-related topics and network with industry-leading member companies. By bringing together a community of well-known technology leaders with vast expertise in firmware development, the UEFI Forum fosters growth and innovation in the global firmware industry.

Please note that this is a member-only event. To learn more about the UEFI Forum and possible membership opportunities visit: https://uefi.org/join.

To stay up-to-date on the latest event details, please visit https://uefi.org/events/upcoming.

To learn more about Aptio V UEFI Firmware from AMI, please visit ami.com/products/aptio-v/.
